product_brief: Cloud-based mobile and desktop messaging app with a focus on security and speed using MTProto.
pqc_capability_description: The document explicitly states reliance on "classical crypto algorithms" (AES, SHA-256, RSA, DH). There is no mention of Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) algorithms, hybrid modes, or migration plans.
crypto_primitives: AES-256, SHA-256, SHA-1 (limited use), RSA, Diffie-Hellman (DH), IGE mode
key_management_model: Client-generated authorization keys stored on device; server-side public RSA key built into client; session-specific keys derived from auth_key and message content.
architecture_type: Custom protocol (MTProto) with client-server encryption and end-to-end encryption layers
signature_schemes: RSA (for server authentication during DH exchange); No specific digital signature scheme listed for message signing, relies on MAC-like verification via SHA-256 of auth_key fragment and plaintext.

product_brief: A messaging and calling platform with built-in security layers, spam detection, and privacy controls.
pqc_capability_description: The document does not mention Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC), specific PQC algorithms, or any migration plans related to quantum resistance. It only mentions general "world-class security" and "layers of protection."
